sec1.title: 5.1 What are Living Organisms Made of?
sec1.content: The cell is the basic structural and functional unit of life. Robert Hooke discovered cells in 1665. All living organisms are made of cells. Cells contain specific cell organelles like the nucleus, mitochondria, plastids, and lysosomes. The plasma membrane is the outermost covering of the cell that permits entry and exit of some materials, hence called selectively permeable. Mitochondria are known as the powerhouses of the cell. They generate energy in the form of ATP (Adenosine Triphosphate) molecules through cellular respiration.
